chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.S-563 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Hearing 1. For orders on office objection No.1 to 4 & reply as flag “A”. 2. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 22.07.2025 Mr. Tariq Mehmood Siddiqui and Ms. Adeela Ansari, Advocates for the petitioner. Mr. Zahoor Shah, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh Mr. K.A. Vaswani, Assistant Advocate General. Mr. Raza Mian, DSP Legal-II, CPO, Karachi. PI Muhammad Nawaz, P.S Mura Bola Khan SIP Mushtaq Ahmed, P.S Jatti..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. It is the case of the petitioner that on 29.06.2025 at village Jatti one Asif made call from his mobile No.0347-2958397 and extended threats to vacate the house, which the petitioner refused, on which the private respondents attacked the house of the petitioner and caused injuries to Mst. Khairan (petitioner) with Axe, whereby her legs were damaged. She was referred to Liyari Medical Hospital wherefrom medical certificate was also issued in her favour which has been annexed with the present petition. Counsel for the petitioner submits that despite that medicolegal certificate, opined injuries of cognizable nature, the SHO of P.S Jatti has refused to record the FIR of the incident so also he is playing at the hands of the private respondents and threatening the petitioner that if she complains anywhere, she shall face the consequences. On notice, Superintendent of Police, Sujawal filed its reply wherein it has been stated that SHO Jatti has recently assumed the charge of P.S Jatti and upon the review of the official record, it has appeared that the petitioner namely Mst.

Counsel for the petitioner submits that despite that medicolegal certificate, opined injuries of cognizable nature, the SHO of P.S Jatti has refused to record the FIR of the incident so also he is playing at the hands of the private respondents and threatening the petitioner that if she complains anywhere, she shall face the consequences. On notice, Superintendent of Police, Sujawal filed its reply wherein it has been stated that SHO Jatti has recently assumed the charge of P.S Jatti and upon the review of the official record, it has appeared that the petitioner namely Mst. Khairan or her any relative have neither visited the police station with any complaint nor have submitted any written application ever at the concerned police station and the police has not caused any harassment to the petitioner. 2 It is very surprising that the SHO of P.S Jattti, who is present in Court, has controverted the stance taken by the SP Sujawal, respondent No.3. He submits that the police station has received a written complaint through TCS from the petitioner. From the facts as narrated in the statement filed by the SP Sujawal it appears that the police is acting in league with the private respondents and this helpless lady is before the Court to seek justice which is being denied deliberately by the official respondents. In the given circumstances, the SP Sujawal is directed to be present in person tomorrow i.e 23.07.2025. The SHO, P.S. Jatti submits that he is ready to record statement of the petitioner but his conduct does not appear to be up to the mark. This Court is showing a reasonable restraint to pass any adverse order against the respondent No.5 till tomorrow. The petitioner complained that they apprehend danger to their lives. It is made clear that in case of any loss to the person and property of the petitioner, the FIR shall be shall be registered against the SHO, P.S Jatti. To come up on 23.07.2025. Office is directed to send a copy of this order by fax today to SP Sujawal. JUDGE Ayaz Gul
